indicators.notes: r=0.215 vs log(views) p=0.002. Questions in first 30s of transcript. Audio proxy signal. Strongest audio predictor found.

indicators.label: Caps Ratio
indicators.notes: r=0.151 vs log(views) p=0.031. Ratio of ALL-CAPS words in transcript. Intensity/emphasis proxy.

indicators.label: I Density
indicators.notes: r=0.040 vs log(views), r=0.325 vs keep. "I" pronoun density — personal narrative drives swipe-in engagement.

indicators.label: Unique Ratio 30S
indicators.notes: r=-0.043 vs log(views), r=-0.299 vs retention. Low vocabulary diversity in first 30s = worse retention. Repetition hurts.

indicators.label: But Density
indicators.notes: r=0.123 vs log(views) p=0.079, r=0.141 vs keep. "But" pivot word density per minute. Narrative pivots signal engagement.

indicators.label: Hook Word First 5S
indicators.notes: r=-0.161 vs log(views) p=0.022. Presence of hook words in first 5s is NEGATIVELY correlated — surprising. Overused hooks may signal generic content.

indicators.label: Superlative Density
indicators.notes: r=-0.055 vs log(views), r=-0.155 vs keep. Superlative overload hurts. Less hyperbole = better hook engagement.

indicators.label: First 30S Wps
indicators.notes: r=-0.036 vs log(views), r=0.174 vs retention, r=-0.126 vs keep. Faster speech = better retention but worse keep. Speed tradeoff.

indicators.label: Question Density Full
indicators.notes: r=-0.070 vs log(views), r=-0.143 vs retention. Full-video question density. Too many questions hurts retention.

indicators.label: Speech Acceleration
indicators.notes: r=0.076 vs log(views), r=0.176 vs retention. Accelerating speech (last 30s faster than first 30s) = better retention.

indicators.label: Avg Question Pos
indicators.notes: r=-0.271 vs log(views) p=0.0001. Average position of questions in video. Front-loaded questions = more views. STRONGEST narrative signal found.

indicators.label: Conflict Q2Q3
indicators.notes: r=0.201 vs log(views) p=0.004. Conflict word count in middle quarters (Q2+Q3). Mid-video tension predicts virality.

indicators.label: First Pivot Pos
indicators.notes: r=-0.179 vs log(views) p=0.011. Position of first "but/however" in transcript. Earlier pivots = more views.

indicators.label: Arc Completeness
indicators.notes: r=0.084 vs log(views). 4-element narrative arc (hook+conflict+escalation+payoff) videos avg 9.2M views vs 2.4M for 1-element arcs. Complete stories win.

indicators.label: Hook Concentration
indicators.notes: r=0.017 vs log(views), r=0.260 vs retention. Hook word density concentrated in Q1 vs rest. Concentrated hooks = better retention.

indicators.label: Narrative Density
indicators.notes: r=0.138 vs log(views) p=0.050. Total narrative markers (hooks+conflicts+resolutions) per word. Denser narratives perform better.

indicators.label: Tension Slope
indicators.notes: r=-0.112 vs log(views), r=0.068 vs retention. Slope of conflict density across quarters. Negative = front-loaded tension (better for views).

indicators.label: Cat Science
indicators.notes: r=0.192 vs log(views) p=0.006. SCIENCE category (experiment/robot/laser/magnetic/AI). 2.17x enriched in 10M+ videos. Top category signal.

indicators.label: Cat Making
indicators.notes: r=0.192 vs log(views) p=0.006. MAKING category (made/built/create). 1.41x enriched in 10M+ videos. Core content identity.

indicators.label: Cat Indestructible
indicators.notes: r=0.154 vs log(views) p=0.028. INDESTRUCTIBLE category. 1.78x enriched in 10M+ videos. Strong virality signal.

indicators.label: Cat Superhero Retention
indicators.notes: r=0.132 vs log(views), r=-0.251 vs retention. SUPERHERO category: good for views but HURTS retention. Clickbait risk.

indicators.label: Cat Money Anti
indicators.notes: r=-0.079 vs log(views). MONEY category: 0.28x enriched in 10M+ (ANTI-signal). Money-focused titles underperform.

indicators.label: Pair Making Danger
indicators.notes: avg_logV=7.246 (n=4). MAKING+DANGER pair is the highest-performing category combination. Making dangerous things = virality formula.

indicators.label: Pair Indestructible Making
indicators.notes: avg_logV=7.087 (n=11). INDESTRUCTIBLE+MAKING pair. Second-best combo. Core Tyler brand.

indicators.label: Pair Making Science
indicators.notes: avg_logV=7.037 (n=8). MAKING+SCIENCE pair. Third-best combo. Science builds perform well.

indicators.label: Pair Making Food
indicators.notes: avg_logV=7.005 (n=6). MAKING+FOOD pair. Food builds cross into 10M territory.

indicators.label: Cat Danger Enrichment
indicators.notes: r=0.078 vs log(views). DANGER category: 1.61x enriched in 10M+. Danger element boosts virality.

indicators.label: Preupload R2
indicators.notes: R²=0.022 from 10 pre-upload signals vs R²=0.219 from keep+retention. Pre-upload signals explain almost nothing. Performance is entirely content-driven.

indicators.label: Title Score Vs Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.265 vs keep. Composite title score (power_words+caps+number+curiosity) NEGATIVELY predicts keep. Clickbait titles backfire on swipe engagement.

indicators.label: Reach Ratio
indicators.notes: r=0.696 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Non-subscriber view fraction. partial_r=0.609 (controlling keep+ret). STRONGEST SIGNAL FOUND. Algorithm distribution proxy.

indicators.label: Biggest Drop Magnitude
indicators.notes: r=-0.394 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Biggest single retention drop. partial_r=-0.315. Smoother curve = more views, independent of keep/retention.

indicators.label: Day1 Frac
indicators.notes: r=-0.371 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Fraction of views on day 1. partial_r=-0.321. SLOW STARTERS WIN — algorithmic distribution builds over days.

indicators.label: Like Ratio
indicators.notes: r=-0.292 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Likes per 1000 views. partial_r=-0.235. Higher like ratio = fewer views. Fans like, viral viewers don't.

indicators.label: Hook Hold Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.234 vs log(views) p=0.0008. partial_r=-0.203. Retention at 10% / start. Counter-intuitive: videos with LARGER initial drop get more views.

indicators.label: Body Hold
indicators.notes: r=0.193 vs log(views) p=0.006. Retention at 50% / retention at 25%. Good mid-body hold predicts views.

indicators.label: Sub Nonsub Gap
indicators.notes: r=-0.224 vs log(views), r=-0.478 vs keep, r=-0.327 vs retention. Gap between sub and non-sub retention. Huge gap = content only works for existing fans.

indicators.label: First3 Frac
indicators.notes: r=-0.224 vs log(views) p=0.001. partial_r=-0.154. First 3 days as fraction of total views. Confirms slow-starter pattern from day1_frac.

indicators.label: Ending Vs Mid
indicators.notes: r=0.154 vs log(views), r=0.354 vs keep. End retention vs mid retention. Strong endings boost keep rate — rewatch/save signal.

indicators.label: Revenue Per View
indicators.notes: r=-0.009 vs log(views), r=0.483 vs keep. Revenue per 1000 views. Monetizable attention correlates with keep, not views.

indicators.label: Comment Ratio
indicators.notes: r=-0.199 vs log(views) p=0.005. partial_r=-0.110. Comments per 1000 views. Like like_ratio — engaged communities comment more per view.

indicators.label: Growth D2 D1
indicators.notes: r=0.138 vs log(views) p=0.050. Day 2 / Day 1 view growth ratio. Growth after day 1 predicts total views.

indicators.label: Swiped Away Rate
indicators.notes: r=0.050 vs log(views), r=-0.389 vs retention. Swipe-away rate hurts retention dramatically but doesn't kill views directly.

indicators.label: Pct 50 Retention
indicators.notes: r=0.246 vs log(views), r=0.832 vs retention. Retention at video midpoint. Nearly identical to avg retention (redundant but validates curve shape).

indicators.label: Keep X Biggest Drop
indicators.notes: r=-0.486 vs log(views). synergy=+0.056. keep × biggest_drop interaction — combined effect stronger than either alone.

indicators.label: Keep X Retention
indicators.notes: r=0.447 vs log(views). synergy=+0.017. keep × retention multiplicative interaction.

indicators.label: First30Q X Conflict
indicators.notes: r=0.236 vs log(views) p=0.001. synergy=+0.021. Questions in first 30s × mid-video conflict count. Combined curiosity signal.

indicators.label: Narrative X Reach
indicators.notes: r=0.338 vs log(views) p<0.001. arc_completeness × reach_ratio. Complete arcs that reach non-subscribers = virality formula.

indicators.label: Reach Per Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.306 vs log(views) p<0.001. reach_ratio / (keep/100). Algorithmic reach normalized by hook quality.

indicators.label: Hook Efficiency
indicators.notes: r=0.264 vs log(views) p=0.0001. keep × vz_score / 10. Hook quality composite.

indicators.label: Question Front Load
indicators.notes: r=0.234 vs log(views) p=0.001. first_30s_questions × (1 - avg_question_pos). Front-loaded curiosity signal.

indicators.label: Pivot Speed X Conflict
indicators.notes: r=0.221 vs log(views) p=0.002. (1 - first_pivot_pos) × conflict_q2q3. Fast pivots with high conflict = views.

indicators.label: Reach Threshold P50
indicators.notes: reach_ratio >= 0.89: Δmean_logV=+1.131 (p<0.001). MASSIVE threshold effect — crossing median reach = 13.5x more views.

indicators.label: Keep Threshold P50
indicators.notes: keep >= 75.7: Δmean_logV=+0.653 (p<0.001). Crossing median keep = 4.5x more views.

indicators.label: Reach Threshold P75
indicators.notes: reach_ratio >= 0.94: Δmean_logV=+1.183 (p<0.001). Top quartile reach = 15.2x more views than bottom 75%.

indicators.label: Is Hook Hold Shape
indicators.notes: r=0.199 vs log(views) p=0.005. HOOK_HOLD retention shape (big drop then flat body). 27 videos. avg_logV=6.672 vs 6.254 for smooth_decline.

indicators.label: Hook Body Ratio
indicators.notes: r=0.348 vs log(views) p<0.001. Ratio of hook drop to body drop in retention curve. Hook hard, hold flat = formula.

indicators.label: Early Drop Positive
indicators.notes: r=0.363 vs log(views) p<0.001. Counter-intuitive: BIGGER early retention drops = MORE views. Algorithm rewards committed watchers.

indicators.label: Avg Concavity
indicators.notes: r=0.233 vs log(views) p=0.001. Positive curve concavity (drops decelerating). Slowing drops = more views.

indicators.label: Body Drop Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.312 vs log(views) p<0.001. Less body drop = more views. Body retention is what the algorithm optimizes for.

indicators.label: Hook Drop Positive
indicators.notes: r=0.326 vs log(views) p<0.001. Bigger hook drops = more views. Confirms HOOK_HOLD pattern — losing viewers early is OK.

indicators.label: Mid Range Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.247 vs log(views) p<0.001. Smaller mid-section range = more views. Flat middle = good.

indicators.label: Avg Concavity Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.524 vs keep. avg_concavity strongly predicts keep — videos where drops slow down get more swipe-in.

indicators.label: Novelty Per Duration
indicators.notes: r=-0.176 vs log(views) p=0.012. Novelty per minute. High novelty density in short videos doesn't help.

indicators.label: Neg Slope
indicators.notes: r=-0.144 vs log(views) p=0.041. Negative sentiment slope across video segments. Decreasing negativity over time predicts views.

indicators.label: Resolution Peak Pos
indicators.notes: r=0.142 vs log(views) p=0.043. Position of peak resolution words. Later resolution = more views. Delayed gratification.

indicators.label: Swing Count
indicators.notes: r=0.139 vs log(views) p=0.048. Number of sentiment swings (sign changes). More emotional variety = more views.

indicators.label: Energy Variance
indicators.notes: r=-0.021 vs log(views), r=0.174 vs retention. Speech pace variance across segments. Consistent pace helps retention.

indicators.label: Sub Lost Ratio
indicators.notes: r=-0.169 vs log(views) p=0.016. Subscriber churn ratio (lost/gained). Higher churn = fewer views. Quality indicator.

indicators.label: Engagement Density
indicators.notes: r=-0.292 vs log(views) p<0.001. Total engagements (likes+shares+comments+subs) per view. Confirms: engaged communities ≠ viral reach.

indicators.label: Rpm Vs Keep
indicators.notes: rpm r=0.483 vs keep. Revenue per 1000 views strongly correlates with keep. Monetizable attention = keep quality.

indicators.label: Share To Like
indicators.notes: r=0.066 vs log(views), r=-0.275 vs retention. Share-to-like ratio. Higher sharing = worse retention (viewers share and leave).

indicators.label: Model R2 Full
indicators.notes: R²=0.668 from 20-feature model. Adj R²=0.632. Full model explains 67% of view variance.

indicators.label: Model Best5
indicators.notes: R²=0.630 from 5 features: reach_ratio + day1_frac + hook_body_ratio + body_drop + first_30s_questions. Parsimonious and powerful.

indicators.label: Model Best3
indicators.notes: R²=0.585 from 3 features: reach_ratio + day1_frac + body_drop. Three signals explain 59% of view variance.

indicators.label: Reach Ratio Incremental
indicators.notes: ΔR²=+0.290 when adding reach_ratio to keep+retention. Single most important signal addition. Goes from R²=0.219 to R²=0.509.

indicators.label: Day1 Frac Incremental
indicators.notes: ΔR²=+0.081 when adding day1_frac to keep+retention. Second-best incremental signal. Slow starts = algorithmic distribution.

indicators.label: Biggest Drop Incremental
indicators.notes: ΔR²=+0.077 when adding biggest_drop to keep+retention. Third-best incremental signal. Retention curve smoothness.

indicators.label: Standardized Importance
indicators.notes: Standardized β ranking: reach_ratio(0.448) > body_drop(-0.300) > hook_body_ratio(-0.259) > day1_frac(-0.147) > keep(0.097). Reach dominates.

indicators.label: Sub Retention Quality
indicators.notes: r=0.169 vs log(views) p=0.016. 1 - sub_lost/sub_gained. Higher quality = more views. Videos that make subs stay perform better.

indicators.label: Week Momentum
indicators.notes: r=0.337 vs log(views) p<0.001. Day 7 views / Day 1 views. Growth across first week predicts total views. Algorithm momentum signal.

indicators.label: Second Wave Ratio
indicators.notes: r=0.204 vs log(views) p=0.004, r=0.243 vs retention. Second peak / first peak after day 7. Re-engagement signal.

indicators.label: Peak Day Idx
indicators.notes: r=0.109 vs log(views), r=0.213 vs retention. Day of peak views. Later peaks suggest algorithmic pickup over time.

indicators.label: Title Linguistics Null
indicators.notes: All title linguistic signals (syllables, readability, phonetics, alliteration) show |r|<0.09 vs views. Confirms: title structure is noise, content is signal.

indicators.label: Upper Ratio Vs Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.192 vs keep. Title uppercase ratio HURTS keep. ALL CAPS in titles backfires on swipe engagement.

indicators.label: Pacing Null
indicators.notes: All video pacing signals (variance, slope, bursts, silence, speech_ratio) show |r|<0.10 vs views. Pacing rhythm does not predict virality.

indicators.label: Info Density Mean
indicators.notes: r=0.073 vs log(views), r=-0.295 vs retention. Longer average word length (complexity proxy) hurts retention.

indicators.label: Tier Reach Threshold
indicators.notes: Tier analysis: 100M+ reach=0.99, 10-50M reach=0.96, 1-10M reach=0.89, 100K-1M reach=0.78. reach_ratio is the tier boundary.

indicators.label: Tier Day1 Frac
indicators.notes: Tier analysis: 100M+ day1=0.03, 50-100M day1=0.01, 1-10M day1=0.03, 100K-1M day1=0.05. Viral videos are slow starters (alg distribution).

indicators.label: Tier Hook Body
indicators.notes: Tier analysis: 10-50M hbr=2.48, 1-10M hbr=2.33, 100K-1M hbr=1.81. Hook-then-hold pattern intensifies with view tier.

indicators.label: Overperformer Profile
indicators.notes: Overperformers (views >> keep+ret prediction): reach=0.95, day1=0.02, body_drop=0.27, hbr=2.47, duration=60s. Algorithmic escape velocity.

indicators.label: Underperformer Profile
indicators.notes: Underperformers (views << prediction): reach=0.78, day1=0.06, body_drop=0.32, hbr=1.84, duration=45s. Trapped in subscriber bubble.

indicators.label: Overperform Duration
indicators.notes: Overperformers are 14.8s LONGER than underperformers (60s vs 45s, p=0.003). Slightly longer = more algorithmic surface area.

indicators.label: Residual Top1
indicators.notes: Making INDESTRUCTIBLE armour overperforms by +1.89 log-orders (78x predicted). INDESTRUCTIBLE+MAKING+high_reach = ultimate formula.

indicators.label: Indestructible Armour 285M
indicators.notes: Top video: 285M views, k=79, ret=87, reach=0.99, day1=0.027, hbr=2.1. Nearly perfect algorithmic video.

indicators.label: Question Q1
indicators.notes: r=0.258 vs log(views) p=0.0002. Question density in first quarter of transcript. STRONG hook signal. Questions in Q1 predict virality.

indicators.label: Hook Word Density
indicators.notes: r=-0.168 vs log(views) p=0.017. Hook keyword density in Q1 NEGATIVELY predicts views. Formulaic hooks backfire.

indicators.label: Conflict Early
indicators.notes: r=0.097 vs log(views) p=0.168. Early conflict introduction (Q1-Q2) weakly positive for views. Tension setup helps.

indicators.label: Excl Trajectory
indicators.notes: r=0.115 vs log(views) p=0.101. Rising exclamation density (end vs start) trends toward more views. Building excitement trajectory.

indicators.label: Q Density 30S
indicators.notes: r=0.223 vs log(views) p=0.001. Question density in first 30 seconds of transcript. Confirms question_q1. Questions early = more views.

indicators.label: Avg Word Len Ret
indicators.notes: r=-0.391 vs retention. Average word length STRONGLY negatively predicts retention. Simpler language = viewers stay longer.

indicators.label: Wps Retention
indicators.notes: r=0.253 vs retention. Words per second (speech pace) positively predicts retention. Faster speech = better retention.

indicators.label: You Density Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.251 vs keep. "You/your" density negatively predicts keep rate. Addressing viewer directly hurts hook.

indicators.label: Repeated Words 30S
indicators.notes: r=0.101 vs log(views), r=0.242 vs retention. Word repetition in first 30s positively predicts retention. Repetition anchors attention.

indicators.label: Is Superhero
indicators.notes: r=0.141 vs log(views) p=0.046. Superhero category binary signal. Superhero content predicts higher views.

indicators.label: Is Physical
indicators.notes: r=0.143 vs log(views) p=0.042. Physical challenge category signal. Physical feats predict higher views.

indicators.label: Is Making
indicators.notes: r=0.147 vs log(views) p=0.037. Making/building category signal predicts higher views.

indicators.label: Making Superhero Synergy
indicators.notes: MAKING+SUPERHERO combo: 2.57x synergy ratio, avg 20.4M views (n=6). Strongest category combination.

indicators.label: Making Physical Synergy
indicators.notes: MAKING+PHYSICAL combo: 1.95x synergy ratio, avg 15.5M views (n=4). Building physical things = viral formula.

indicators.label: Indestructible Category
indicators.notes: INDESTRUCTIBLE category: avg 38.8M views (n=9). Highest performing single category by far.

indicators.label: Preupload R2 Null
indicators.notes: Pre-upload signals alone: R²=0.066, CV R²=-0.13. Cannot predict views from pre-upload signals. Post-upload performance (keep, retention, reach) dominates.

indicators.label: Duration Views
indicators.notes: r=0.196 vs log(views) p=0.005. Longer videos get more views. Only pre-upload signal with strong predictive power.

indicators.label: Power Word Count
indicators.notes: r=0.121 vs log(views) p=0.086. Title power word count weakly positive. Superlative language in titles helps marginally.

indicators.label: Vz Type C Best
indicators.notes: VZ type C: avg 9.3M views (n=119) vs type B: 6.1M (n=75), type A: 4.8M (n=7). Type C visual Zeigarnik most effective.

indicators.label: Opening Wps 3S
indicators.notes: r=-0.216 vs log(views) p=0.002, r=-0.370 vs keep. Words per second in first 3 seconds. SLOWER openings = more views + better keep. Counter-intuitive.

indicators.label: First Sentence Len
indicators.notes: r=-0.044 vs log(views), r=0.338 vs retention. Longer first sentences = better retention. Build context before action.

indicators.label: Open Word These
indicators.notes: Opening with "these" (n=10): avg logV=6.701. Opening with "how" (n=4): avg logV=6.752. Best opening words introduce subject/question.

indicators.label: Open Word I Weak
indicators.notes: Opening with "I" (n=46): avg logV=6.157. Weakest common opener. Despite i_density correlating with keep, "I" openings underperform.

indicators.label: Gini Retention
indicators.notes: r=-0.396 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Gini coefficient of retention curve. More unequal retention = fewer views. Flat curves win.

indicators.label: Hook Sharpness
indicators.notes: r=-0.348 vs log(views) p<0.0001, r=-0.502 vs retention. Slope of first 5% of retention. Sharper hooks HURT. Gentle hooks retain better.

indicators.label: Q2 Momentum
indicators.notes: r=0.308 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Retention momentum at 20-40% mark. CRITICAL window — gaining retention here predicts virality.

indicators.label: Q3 Momentum
indicators.notes: r=0.314 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Retention momentum at 40-60% mark. Mid-video retention gains are the strongest predictor among quintile signals.

indicators.label: Entropy Retention
indicators.notes: r=-0.335 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Shannon entropy of retention curve bins. Lower entropy = more uniform = more views.

indicators.label: Biggest Cliff 2Nd
indicators.notes: r=-0.405 vs log(views) p<0.0001. Confirms biggest_drop finding with higher precision. Single biggest cliff drop in retention kills views.

indicators.label: Mean D2 Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.229 vs log(views) p=0.001, r=0.522 vs keep. Mean second derivative (concavity). Decelerating drops = better keep + more views.

indicators.label: Cliff Position
indicators.notes: r=-0.039 vs log(views), r=-0.363 vs keep. Where the biggest cliff occurs. Earlier cliff = worse keep. Cliff position matters for swipe.

indicators.label: Q1 Momentum Neg
indicators.notes: r=-0.182 vs log(views) p=0.009. Q1 (first 20%) negative momentum = more views. LOSING viewers early is OK if you hold later.

indicators.label: Q5 Momentum Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.070 vs log(views), r=0.420 vs keep. Ending momentum (80-100%) strongly predicts keep. Strong endings = re-swipe.

indicators.label: Terminal Slope Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.073 vs log(views), r=0.330 vs keep. Last 20% retention slope. Positive terminal slope = strong keep. Ending on a high = replay.

indicators.label: Cv Overfit Warning
indicators.notes: Cross-validation: reach_ratio model R²=0.594 train but 0.122 test. MASSIVE overfit. reach_ratio is consequence, not cause. Test R² is the real number.

indicators.label: Causal Keep Reaches
indicators.notes: keep is the #1 predictor of reach_ratio (r=0.476). Higher keep → algorithm distributes wider. KEEP IS THE MASTER LEVER.

indicators.label: Causal Hbr Reach
indicators.notes: hook_body_ratio predicts reach r=0.432, partial_r=0.339 (controlling keep+ret). Hook-then-hold shape INDEPENDENTLY drives algorithmic pickup.

indicators.label: Causal Duration Reach
indicators.notes: duration predicts reach r=0.248, partial_r=0.330. Longer videos (within Shorts) get independently more algorithmic distribution.

indicators.label: Causal Questions Reach
indicators.notes: avg_question_pos predicts reach r=-0.201, partial_r=-0.184. Front-loaded questions independently help algorithmic distribution.

indicators.label: Causal Conflict Reach
indicators.notes: conflict_q2q3 predicts reach r=0.175, partial_r=0.158. Mid-video conflict words independently help algorithmic distribution.

indicators.label: Views Beyond Reach Gini
indicators.notes: gini partial_r(views|reach)=-0.383. Flat retention curves add views BEYOND algorithmic reach. Independent content quality signal.

indicators.label: Views Beyond Reach Q2
indicators.notes: q2_momentum partial_r(views|reach)=0.323. Mid-video retention gains add views beyond reach. The algorithm rewards mid-video engagement.

indicators.label: Views Beyond Reach Body
indicators.notes: body_drop partial_r(views|reach)=-0.289. Less body drop adds views beyond reach. Body retention is independently valuable.

indicators.label: Cv Best Content Model
indicators.notes: Best cross-validated content model: keep+ret+body_drop+hbr+q2_momentum+gini. Test R²=0.176, 38% improvement over keep+retention alone.

indicators.label: Cv Content Ceiling
indicators.notes: Content-only models max at Test R²≈0.18. ~82% of view variance is unexplained by content signals. External factors (timing, audience, algorithm state) dominate.

indicators.label: Opening Wps Beyond Reach
indicators.notes: opening_wps_3s partial_r(views|reach)=-0.171. Slower openings add views even controlling for reach. Pace restraint is independently valuable.

indicators.label: Arc Beyond Reach
indicators.notes: arc_completeness partial_r(views|reach)=0.142 (p=0.043). Complete narrative arcs add views beyond algorithmic reach.

indicators.label: Scale Factor Vision
indicators.notes: r=0.355 vs log(views) p=0.012. Scale of objects in first 5 frames (GPT-4o-mini scored). Impressive scale = more views. STRONG visual signal.

indicators.label: Color Contrast Vision
indicators.notes: r=+0.167 vs log(views), p=0.017. Color contrast in first 5 frames is the only significant visual signal. Also r=+0.236 vs keep — striking colors help click-through.

indicators.label: Environment Novelty Vision
indicators.notes: r=0.305 vs log(views) p=0.031. Environment novelty in first 5 frames. Unusual/unexpected settings predict views.

indicators.label: Spectacle Score Vision
indicators.notes: r=0.307 vs log(views) p=0.030. Composite: (scale+environment_novelty+action)/3. Overall spectacle predicts views.

indicators.label: Visual Impact Vision
indicators.notes: r=0.289 vs log(views) p=0.042. Composite: (action+scale+color)/3. Overall visual impact predicts views.

indicators.label: Expression Intensity Vision
indicators.notes: r=0.091 vs log(views) NS, but r=0.225 vs retention. Facial expression intensity helps retention but not views.

indicators.label: Keep Predictor Concavity
indicators.notes: early_concavity r=0.444 vs keep (p<0.001). Decelerating early retention drops = best keep predictor. Gentle landing after hook.

indicators.label: Keep Predictor Opening Pace
indicators.notes: opening_wps_3s r=-0.370 vs keep (p<0.001). Slower first 3 seconds = better keep. Don't rush the opening.

indicators.label: Keep Predictor Early Slope
indicators.notes: early_slope r=-0.345 vs keep (p<0.001). Less steep initial retention slope = better keep. Gentle curve = viewer commits.

indicators.label: Keep Predictor I Density
indicators.notes: i_density r=0.325 vs keep (p<0.001). "I" pronoun usage. Personal narrative drives swipe-in engagement.

indicators.label: Keep Predictor Duration
indicators.notes: duration_s r=0.231 vs keep (p=0.001). Longer Shorts have better keep. More content = more reason to stay.

indicators.label: Keep Prediction Ceiling
indicators.notes: Cross-validated keep prediction: best model Test R²=0.055. Keep is 94.5% unpredictable from content signals. Dominated by thumbnail/first-frame visual impact.

indicators.label: Keep Vz Score
indicators.notes: vz_score r=0.224 vs keep (p=0.001). Visual Zeigarnik (curiosity) in first 3 frames predicts keep. Thumbnail curiosity gap works.

indicators.label: Early Concavity Views
indicators.notes: early_concavity r=0.414 vs log(views). Top of ALL content signals for views. Gentle post-hook deceleration predicts virality.

indicators.label: Vision Color Contrast
indicators.notes: r=0.075 vs log(views), r=0.265 vs keep. GPT-4o-mini vision: color contrast in first 5 frames. Vibrant colors drive keep.

indicators.label: Vision Environment Novelty
indicators.notes: r=0.046 vs log(views), r=0.202 vs keep. Visual environment novelty. Novel settings drive keep but not views directly.

indicators.label: Vision Action Intensity
indicators.notes: r=0.077 vs log(views), r=0.186 vs keep. Physical action in opening frames. More action = better keep.

indicators.label: Vision Scale Factor
indicators.notes: r=0.077 vs log(views), r=0.171 vs keep. Visual scale/impressiveness. Bigger subjects = better keep.

indicators.label: Vision Text Overlay
indicators.notes: r=0.118 vs log(views) p=0.095. Text overlays in first 5 frames. Mild positive correlation with views.

indicators.label: Vision Complexity Neg
indicators.notes: r=-0.094 vs log(views). Visual complexity HURTS views. Clean, simple visuals outperform busy frames.

indicators.label: Vision Null Expressions
indicators.notes: expression_intensity r=0.048 vs views, r=0.048 vs keep. Facial expressions don't significantly predict either. Content > reactions.

indicators.label: Vision Null Person
indicators.notes: person_prominence r=0.040 vs views. Person in frame doesn't predict views. Object/action-focused thumbnails work equally well.

indicators.label: Keep Times Retention
indicators.notes: r=0.447 vs log(views) p<0.001. keep × retention combined signal. STRONGEST ratio signal. Hook quality × body quality = viral power.

indicators.label: Wps Times Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.249 vs log(views) p=0.0003. Speech pace × keep rate. Fast pace + good hook = views.

indicators.label: Hook Body Gap
indicators.notes: r=-0.228 vs log(views) p=0.001. Gap between 3s retention and midpoint retention. Smaller gap = more views. Consistent retention beats frontloaded hooks.

indicators.label: Q Density 30S X Wps
indicators.notes: r=-0.235 vs log(views) p=0.0007. Questions × speech pace interaction NEGATIVE. Questions work better at SLOWER pace. Let questions breathe.

indicators.label: Q Density 30S X Drop
indicators.notes: r=0.200 vs log(views) p=0.004. Questions × biggest retention drop interaction POSITIVE. Questions paired with dramatic drops predict views.

indicators.label: Keep Squared
indicators.notes: r=0.442 vs log(views) p<0.001. Squared keep rate. Nonlinear relationship: keep² predicts better than linear keep. Diminishing returns below 70%, accelerating above.

indicators.label: Biggest Drop Squared
indicators.notes: r=0.382 vs log(views) p<0.001. Squared biggest retention drop. Nonlinear: extreme drops disproportionately predict views.

indicators.label: Log Duration
indicators.notes: r=0.222 vs log(views) p=0.002. Log of duration. Logarithmic relationship: doubling duration adds fixed view increment. Diminishing returns.

indicators.label: Q Per Keep
indicators.notes: r=0.204 vs log(views) p=0.004. Question density / keep rate. Questions adjusted for hook quality. Questions add value beyond their hook-boosting effect.

indicators.label: Drop Per Duration
indicators.notes: r=0.150 vs log(views) p=0.033. Biggest retention drop / duration × 100. Drop intensity relative to video length. Concentrated drama helps.

indicators.label: Novelty X You Density
indicators.notes: r=0.163 vs log(views) p=0.020. Novelty × viewer-address interaction. Novel content + addressing viewer = more views. Novel concepts benefit from direct address.

indicators.label: Kmeans Best Cluster
indicators.notes: K-means k=3 retention clusters. Best cluster (n=92): avg_logV=6.491, keep=77.2, ret=87.6, drop=0.584, flat_mid=0.028. Gentle-decline profile.

indicators.label: Is Best Cluster Signal
indicators.notes: is_best_cluster (k=3): r=0.231 vs log(views) p=0.001, r=0.314 vs keep. Membership in gentle-decline retention cluster predicts views.

indicators.label: Worst Cluster Profile
indicators.notes: Worst cluster (n=71): avg_logV=6.139, keep=73.5, ret=75.0, drop=0.672. Steep-decline profile with low overall retention.

indicators.label: Quadratic Model
indicators.notes: Quadratic (keep+ret+squares+interaction): R²=0.243 vs linear R²=0.219. Mild non-linear improvement. Adding body_drop quadratic: R²=0.291.

indicators.label: Keep Threshold 74
indicators.notes: Optimal keep threshold at 74: Δ=+0.674 logV (p<0.001). Videos with keep≥74 average 4.7x more views. Key threshold for algorithmic pickup.

indicators.label: Keep Threshold 68
indicators.notes: Keep≥68: Δ=+0.448 logV (p=0.0004). Critical minimum threshold — below 68 keep, videos rarely break out.

indicators.label: Keep Threshold 80
indicators.notes: Keep≥80: Δ=+0.571 logV (p<0.001), n=55 videos. Entering elite territory — above 80 keep rarely fails.

indicators.label: Kmeans K4 High Ret
indicators.notes: K-means k=4: high-retention cluster (n=47): avg_logV=6.359, ret=89.0, drop=0.573. Highest retention = most holding power.

indicators.label: Ttr Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.182 vs log(views) p=0.009, r=-0.300 vs keep. Type-token ratio (vocabulary diversity). MORE repetition = better. Consistent message > varied vocabulary.

indicators.label: Discourse Density
indicators.notes: r=0.151 vs log(views) p=0.032, r=0.303 vs keep. Discourse markers (so/but/actually/basically). Conversational connectors drive keep.

indicators.label: Sensory Density
indicators.notes: r=0.098 vs log(views), r=0.323 vs retention. Sensory words (see/feel/hear/touch). Sensory language keeps people watching.

indicators.label: Second Person Neg
indicators.notes: r=-0.091 vs log(views), r=-0.222 vs keep. "You/your" addressing viewer directly HURTS keep. Talk about yourself, not the audience.

indicators.label: Viral Word Material
indicators.notes: material 14x enriched in 10M+ videos. Material science testing language is Tyler's viral signature.

indicators.label: Viral Word Strong
indicators.notes: strong 7.78x enriched in 10M+ videos. "indestructible" 6.22x. Strength-testing content = virality formula.

indicators.label: Death Word Today
indicators.notes: today appears 0 times in 10M+ videos but 13 times in sub-1M. "today" = death sentence. Urgency/timeliness kills virality.

indicators.label: Death Word People
indicators.notes: people appears 0 times in 10M+ but 8x in sub-1M. Generic references kill. Be specific about materials, not people.

indicators.label: Hapax Ratio
indicators.notes: r=-0.083 vs views, r=-0.193 vs keep. Hapax legomena (unique words). Fewer unique words = better keep. Repetition reinforces engagement.

indicators.label: Second Wave
indicators.notes: r=0.407 vs log(views) p<0.001. Binary: any day after day 7 with >50% of day 1 views. STRONGEST daily trajectory signal. Algorithmic re-promotion indicator.

indicators.label: Accel Ratio
indicators.notes: r=0.249 vs log(views) p=0.0004. Day 2-3 avg views / day 1 views. Early acceleration predicts total views. Algorithm picks up on momentum.

indicators.label: Decay Rate Exp
indicators.notes: r=0.232 vs log(views) p=0.001. Exponential decay rate (slope of log-views over first 30 days). Slower decay = more total views. Longevity signal.

indicators.label: Max Growth Rate
indicators.notes: r=0.161 vs log(views) p=0.023. Maximum single-day growth rate in first 14 days. Viral spikes predict total views.

indicators.label: Trajectory Slow Build
indicators.notes: SLOW_BUILD archetype: n=63, avg 10.9M views, ret=85.7%, keep=77.0%. Best performing trajectory. Videos that peak AFTER day 3 with >1.5x acceleration.

indicators.label: Trajectory Evergreen
indicators.notes: EVERGREEN archetype: n=27, avg 11.0M views, ret=80.5%, keep=71.0%. Slow accumulation over 14+ days to 50%. Lower retention but sustained interest.

indicators.label: Initial Drop Ret
indicators.notes: r=0.386 vs log(views) p<0.001. Initial retention drop (first 5% of video). PARADOX: bigger drops predict more views. Captures replay-worthy openings (start >100% from replays, then drop).

indicators.label: Mid Ret Curve
indicators.notes: r=0.291 vs log(views) p<0.001. Midpoint retention from curve. Higher midpoint = more views. Body quality signal.

indicators.label: Smoothness Curve
indicators.notes: r=0.211 vs log(views) p=0.003. Retention curve noise (std of second differences). Noisier curves = more views. Engagement volatility is a feature not a bug.

indicators.label: Cliff Count
indicators.notes: r=0.215 vs log(views) p=0.002. Count of >10% retention drops. More cliffs = more views. Dramatic content has sharp attention shifts.

indicators.label: Curve Type Cliff
indicators.notes: CLIFF curve type: n=136 (67%), avg 11.1M views. DOMINANT archetype. Videos with major retention drops vastly outperform smooth curves (1.4M avg).

indicators.label: End Ret Curve
indicators.notes: r=0.195 vs log(views) p=0.006, r=0.454 vs keep. End retention from curve. Strong ending = better keep rate AND more views.

indicators.label: Slope Second Half
indicators.notes: r=0.035 vs views NS, but r=0.403 vs keep. Second-half retention slope strongly predicts keep rate. Maintaining retention in back half drives swiped-to-next.

indicators.label: Engagement Depth
indicators.notes: r=-0.129 vs log(views), r=-0.471 vs retention, r=-0.428 vs keep. How far below initial retention dips. Deep engagement drops hurt across all metrics.

indicators.label: Drop Position
indicators.notes: r=-0.039 vs views, r=-0.363 vs keep. Position of biggest drop in video. Earlier drops = worse keep rate. Late drops less damaging.

indicators.label: Drop Time Pct Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.039 vs log(views), r=-0.363 vs keep. Retention curve drop position. Earlier biggest drops = worse keep. Late drops are tolerable.

indicators.label: Avg Pivot Effect Ret
indicators.notes: r=-0.045 vs log(views), r=0.267 vs retention. Retention change after "but/however" pivots. Positive pivot effects = better retention.

indicators.label: Log Virality
indicators.notes: r=0.798 vs log(views) p<0.001. log10(shares × views / subs_gained). Viral coefficient. Nearly perfectly correlated with views but circular.

indicators.label: Ret Keep Product
indicators.notes: r=0.447 vs log(views). keep × retention / 100 composite. Slight improvement over keep alone (+0.017). Combined hook+body signal.

indicators.label: Explosion Ratio
indicators.notes: r=-0.246 vs log(views) p=0.0004. Max daily views / mean daily views. Lower explosion = more even distribution = more total views.

indicators.label: View Consistency
indicators.notes: r=-0.135 vs log(views) p=0.055. Daily view std / mean. Lower variability = more consistent algorithmic distribution.

indicators.label: Comment Per Like
indicators.notes: r=-0.136 vs log(views), r=-0.246 vs retention. Comment-to-like ratio. More comments per like = less viral (controversial ≠ viral).

indicators.label: Share Per Like Ret
indicators.notes: r=0.066 vs log(views), r=-0.275 vs retention. Share-to-like ratio. Higher sharing = worse retention (share-and-leave behavior).

indicators.label: Speech Ret Corr
indicators.notes: speech_retention_corr r=-0.093 vs views, r=-0.186 vs retention. Within-video correlation between speech pace and retention. Misalignment detected.

indicators.label: Keep Linear Returns
indicators.notes: Keep returns are LINEAR: low→mid +0.421 logV, mid→high +0.391 logV. No diminishing returns — every keep point matters equally.

indicators.label: Retention Diminishing
indicators.notes: Retention has mild diminishing returns: low→mid +0.315, mid→high +0.241. First gains matter more.

indicators.label: Body Drop Diminishing
indicators.notes: body_drop shows STRONG diminishing returns: first tertile drop = -0.370 logV, top tertile = only -0.164. Getting body_drop below 0.3 is the key threshold.

indicators.label: Conflict Diminishing
indicators.notes: conflict_q2q3 has DIMINISHING returns: low→mid +0.222, mid→high +0.099. 2-3 conflict words is enough — more doesn't help much.

indicators.label: Action Accelerating
indicators.notes: action_density has ACCELERATING returns: low→mid +0.149, mid→high +0.193. More action verbs keep getting better. No ceiling found.

indicators.label: Best Amplifier Pair
indicators.notes: retention×conflict_q2q3 has highest synergy (+0.062 R²). Adding conflict to retention model adds more than to any other signal.

indicators.label: Keep Early Concav Amp
indicators.notes: keep×early_concavity synergy = +0.062 R². These two signals amplify each other — keep AND gentle curves together = multiplicative effect.

indicators.label: Retention Action Amp
indicators.notes: retention×action_density synergy = +0.059 R². Action verbs amplify retention's effect on views.

indicators.label: Opening Pace Nonlinear
indicators.notes: opening_wps_3s: low→mid -0.222, mid→high -0.152. Slower opening helps linearly but with mild diminishing returns. Sweet spot: 3-4 WPS.

indicators.label: Synthesis Tier1
indicators.notes: TIER 1 CAUSAL LEVERS: keep(r=0.476 vs reach), hook_body_ratio(partial_r=0.339), duration(partial_r=0.330), opening_pace(r=-0.370 vs keep).

indicators.label: Synthesis Tier2
indicators.notes: TIER 2 QUALITY: gini(partial_r=-0.383), q2q3_momentum(+0.323), body_drop(-0.289), action_density(0.233), arc(0.142). Independent of reach.

indicators.label: Synthesis Formula
indicators.notes: FORMULA: Virality = keep × (1 + hbr/5) × (1 - body_drop) + bonuses for action>2%, conflict≥2, duration≥50s. Penalty: fast opening, body_drop>0.3.

indicators.label: Synthesis Ceiling
indicators.notes: CEILING: Content signals max at Test R²=0.176 (cross-validated). 82% of view variance is external. Content gives edge, not guarantee.

indicators.label: Synthesis Nulls
indicators.notes: NULLS: Title linguistics, pacing rhythm, pre-upload signals, expressions, person prominence, sentiment, upload day, hero's journey — all negligible.

indicators.label: Indestructible Category Lift
indicators.notes: r=0.244 vs log(views), p<0.001. INDESTRUCTIBLE videos avg 60.9M views (+668% lift). 7 videos, mean keep 80.1%. Strongest single content category.

indicators.label: Making Base Category
indicators.notes: r=0.272 vs log(views), p<0.001. MAKING videos avg 18.3M (+131% lift). 40 videos, keep 76.8%. Most versatile category — creates synergies with others.

indicators.label: Magnets Category Lift
indicators.notes: r=0.210 vs log(views), p=0.003. MAGNETS avg 38.1M (+380% lift). Only 3 videos but consistently massive. keep 78.8%.

indicators.label: Superhero Category
indicators.notes: r=0.159 vs log(views). SUPERHERO videos avg 25.0M (+216% lift). 5 videos, keep 76.1%. Batman/Iron Man concepts resonate.

indicators.label: Challenge Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.175 vs log(views), p=0.012. CHALLENGE framing avg 420K views (-94.7% vs mean). Worst keep rate 67.8%. Avoid challenge framing.

indicators.label: Indestructible X Making Combo
indicators.notes: INDESTRUCTIBLE+MAKING = 143.8M mean views (18× dataset avg). +136% lift vs best individual. The ultimate concept combination.

indicators.label: Food X Making Synergy
indicators.notes: FOOD+MAKING has +0.46 log-views interaction effect (n=6). Strongest true synergy — combo outperforms sum of parts. Mean 20.9M views.

indicators.label: Making X Money Synergy
indicators.notes: MAKING+MONEY has +0.40 log-views interaction effect (n=6). Second strongest synergy. Mean 20.9M views.

indicators.label: Walking X Making Dilution
indicators.notes: MAKING+WALKING has -0.69 interaction effect — strongest dilution. Overlapping physical concepts don't combine well. Also SHOES+WALKING = -0.31.

indicators.label: Shoes Category
indicators.notes: r=0.191 vs log(views), p=0.006. SHOES/WEARABLE avg 11.0M (+38% lift). 21 videos, keep 78.6% (+5.4%). Consistent mid-tier performer.

indicators.label: Questions First30S
indicators.notes: r=+0.215 vs log(views), p=0.002. Videos opening with questions get more views. Strongest audio proxy signal for views.

indicators.label: You Density Hurts Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.298 vs keep, p=0.00002. "You" density (addressing viewer directly) correlates with LOWER keep rate. Generic/clickbaity tone signal.

indicators.label: Wps First10S Slow Better
indicators.notes: r=-0.289 vs keep, p=0.00003. Slower speaking pace in first 10 seconds = better keep. Fast opening loses people.

indicators.label: Lexical Diversity Hurts Retention
indicators.notes: r=-0.333 vs retention, p=0.000001. STRONGEST signal found. Complex vocabulary in first 30s kills retention. Simple, repetitive language retains better.

indicators.label: Key Word Repetition Helps
indicators.notes: r=+0.230 vs retention, p=0.001. Repeating key words 3+ times in first 30s helps retention. Hammering key terms keeps viewers engaged.

indicators.label: Wps Overall Helps Retention
indicators.notes: r=+0.258 vs retention, p=0.0002. Faster overall pace helps retention. Combined with slow opening finding: start measured, then accelerate.

indicators.label: I Count Views
indicators.notes: r=+0.167 vs log(views), p=0.018. First-person "I" references correlate with more views. Personal framing resonates.

indicators.label: Pace Paradox
indicators.notes: Opening pace hurts keep (r=-0.289) but overall pace helps retention (r=+0.258). ACTIONABLE: start slow/measured in first 10s, then accelerate through the body.

indicators.label: Total Conflict Views
indicators.notes: r=+0.264 vs log(views), p=0.00014. Total conflict words (challenge+danger) in transcript strongly predicts views. More conflict = more views.

indicators.label: Arc Score Composite
indicators.notes: r=+0.253 vs log(views), p=0.00027. Composite narrative arc (hook+conflict+escalation+payoff) is a strong predictor. 3/4 completeness = 13M avg vs 1.4M for 0/4.

indicators.label: Hook Question Narrative
indicators.notes: r=+0.213 vs log(views), p=0.002. Videos starting with a question avg 2.3× more views (log 6.586 vs 6.227). Confirms audio proxy finding.

indicators.label: Hook Pace Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.313 vs keep, p<0.00001. STRONGEST keep signal from narrative: slower hooks = higher keep. Confirms pace_paradox from audio analysis.

indicators.label: Conflict Density Signal
indicators.notes: r=+0.179 vs log(views), p=0.011. Conflict words per 100 words matters independently. Also r=+0.215 vs keep, p=0.002.

indicators.label: Danger Vs Challenge Split
indicators.notes: Conflict words drive views (r=+0.264) but hurt retention (r=-0.244). DANGER words specifically help retention (r=+0.212). Challenge creates clicks; danger sustains engagement.

indicators.label: Narrative Completeness Tiers
indicators.notes: 0/4 elements=1.4M avg, 1/4=4.3M, 2/4=6.2M, 3/4=13.0M, 4/4=11.8M. Clear staircase up to 3/4. Diminishing returns at full completeness.

indicators.label: Escalation Stages
indicators.notes: escalation_stages r=-0.184 vs retention, p=0.009. More escalation stages hurt retention — suggests longer escalation = attention fatigue. Keep escalation tight.

indicators.label: Hook Tension Paradox
indicators.notes: hook_tension_count: r=+0.145 vs views but r=-0.248 vs retention. Tension in hook drives clicks but hurts completion. Tension hooks attract but can also repel.

indicators.label: Day1 Pct Flash In Pan
indicators.notes: r=-0.371 vs log(views). STRONGEST trajectory signal. High day1_pct = flash-in-the-pan. Top videos: 2-3% on day1, bottom: 9-11%. Subs-only vs algorithm-pushed.

indicators.label: Day1 Views Raw
indicators.notes: r=+0.346 vs log(views). Raw day-1 volume still matters — a strong absolute launch helps even if proportionally small.

indicators.label: Day7 Vs Day1 Decay
indicators.notes: r=+0.336 vs log(views). Slow decay ratio (day7/day1) strongly predicts total views. Top videos: 0.7-17× day1 at day7. Bottom: 0.06-0.18×.

indicators.label: Acceleration 1 To 3
indicators.notes: r=+0.306 vs log(views). Views INCREASING day1→3 predicts virality. Top: 3-13× growth. Bottom: 0.3-0.5× decay. Algorithm amplification signature.

indicators.label: Avg Wm Per View Day1
indicators.notes: r=+0.310 vs log(views), r=+0.269 vs keep. Day-1 watch-minutes-per-view predicts both views AND keep. Early engagement quality = algorithm signal.

indicators.label: Half Life Signal
indicators.notes: r=+0.296 vs log(views), r=+0.209 vs keep, r=+0.236 vs retention. Longer half-life predicts all three outcomes. Sustained attention = compound growth.

indicators.label: First3 Pct Anti Retention
indicators.notes: r=-0.390 vs retention. STRONGEST retention anti-signal from trajectory. Frontloaded views = low retention. Videos that spread viewership over time retain better.

indicators.label: Day30 Vs Day1 Longevity
indicators.notes: r=+0.311 vs log(views). Day30/day1 ratio = longevity signal. Evergreen content keeps delivering views weeks later.

indicators.label: Viral Trajectory Pattern
indicators.notes: MEGA-HIT TRAJECTORY: peak day 3-6, acceleration 3-13×, half-life 2-3 days, long-tail 27-41%. FLOP: peak day 2, acceleration 0.3×, half-life 1 day, long-tail 51-60%.

indicators.label: Second Peak Null
indicators.notes: has_second_peak and is_frontloaded are ~uncorrelated with views (r≈0). Continuous decay-rate metrics outperform binary shape categories. But second_peak helps retention (r=+0.22).

indicators.label: Sub View Ratio Mega Signal
indicators.notes: r=-0.847 vs log(views), p<0.0001. STRONGEST SIGNAL IN ENTIRE DATASET. High subscriber view fraction = fewer total views. Reach beyond subs is what drives views. Q4-Q1 gap = ~40× views.

indicators.label: Non Sub Ratio
indicators.notes: r=+0.696 vs log(views), p<0.0001. Mirror of sub_view_ratio. Non-subscriber reach fraction is the #2 strongest signal. Viral = reaching beyond your base.

indicators.label: Like Rate Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.292 vs log(views), p<0.0001. High like_rate = FEWER views. Counterintuitive: smaller videos have more engaged core. Viral dilutes per-view engagement.

indicators.label: Engagement Score Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.297 vs log(views), p<0.0001. Weighted engagement score negatively predicts views. Same dilution effect — viral reach trades off vs per-view engagement.

indicators.label: Retention Variation Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.267 vs log(views), p=0.0001. Lower retention variation = more views. Smooth, consistent retention curve beats spiky engagement.

indicators.label: Likes Per Comment Positive
indicators.notes: r=+0.260 vs log(views), r=+0.410 vs retention. High likes-to-comments ratio = broad passive approval. Low ratio = divisive/niche content.

indicators.label: Sub Vs Nonsub Retention Quality
indicators.notes: r=-0.478 vs keep, r=-0.327 vs retention. When subs watch much longer than non-subs = fan service underperforming with general audience. Best videos have EQUAL sub/nonsub retention.

indicators.label: Comment Rate Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.199 vs log(views). Q1 (lowest comment rate) avg 5.3M views vs Q4 (highest) 760K — 7× gap. Comment-heavy = niche.

indicators.label: Rpm Predicts Keep Not Views
indicators.notes: rpm r≈0 vs views but r=+0.477 vs keep. Higher RPM videos have better retention metrics but don't necessarily get more views. Quality without reach.

indicators.label: Engagement Dilution Law
indicators.notes: PATTERN: Per-view engagement metrics (like_rate, comment_rate, engagement_score) ALL negatively correlate with views. Viral reach dilutes engagement rates. Don't optimize for engagement rate — optimize for reach.

indicators.label: Preupload Ceiling
indicators.notes: Pre-upload signals explain only CV R²=0.031 (3% of view variance). Cannot predict if video gets 200K or 20M from pre-upload alone. 97% of variance is external/post-upload.

indicators.label: Action Verb Title
indicators.notes: r=+0.227 vs log(views). Action verbs in title (Making, Building, Testing) are the #1 pre-upload title signal. Strongest individual pre-upload predictor.

indicators.label: Log Duration Preupload
indicators.notes: r=+0.222 vs log(views). Longer videos get more views. Survives stepwise selection. Coefficient +1.776 in final formula.

indicators.label: Unique Word Ratio Negative
indicators.notes: r=-0.177 vs log(views). Lower vocabulary diversity = more views. Simple, repetitive language wins. Confirms lexical_diversity finding from audio analysis.

indicators.label: Title Sentiment Positive
indicators.notes: r=+0.125 vs log(views). Positive title sentiment helps. Survives stepwise with coefficient +0.325. Fourth of only 4 pre-upload signals that survive CV.

indicators.label: Preupload Formula
indicators.notes: FORMULA: log10(views) = 2.74 + 1.78*log_duration + 0.36*has_action_verb + 0.55*is_short + 0.33*title_sentiment. Only 4 features survive honest CV. Tier accuracy 66%.

indicators.label: Zeigarnik Null Preupload
indicators.notes: z_score r=-0.076, vz_score r=+0.016, novelty r=-0.006 vs log(views). Zeigarnik, novelty, and cognitive_load are NOT predictive of views pre-upload. Hard null.

indicators.label: Preupload Overpredictions
indicators.notes: Most over-predicted: action titles with low views (Eating 10K cals, Best LIAR, Solar Death Ray). Format matches pattern but content didn't resonate. Pre-upload can't measure resonance.

indicators.label: Scale Factor Marginal
indicators.notes: r=+0.130 vs log(views), p=0.066. Scale/impressiveness of main object is marginally significant. Bigger setups trend toward more views but borderline.

indicators.label: Action Intensity Null
indicators.notes: r=+0.059 vs log(views), p=0.403. Visual action intensity in first 5 frames is NOT predictive. Movement in opening frames doesn't drive views.

indicators.label: Expression Intensity Null
indicators.notes: r=+0.067 vs log(views), p=0.346. Facial expression intensity is NOT predictive. Confirms prior finding that person prominence doesn't matter.

indicators.label: Environment Novelty Null
indicators.notes: r=+0.006 vs log(views), p=0.933. Environment novelty scored by GPT-4o-mini vision is completely null. Setting doesn't predict views.

indicators.label: Visual Signals Weak
indicators.notes: Of 5 GPT-4o-mini vision signals, only color_contrast is significant. Visual content analysis via AI scoring adds little predictive power. Content > visuals.

indicators.label: First Half Vs Second Half
indicators.notes: r=-0.396 vs log(views). STRONGEST retention shape signal. Videos where second half retains proportionally more audience get far more views. Balanced curves beat front-loaded.

indicators.label: Body Stability
indicators.notes: r=+0.365 vs log(views). Consistent flat body retention (low variance 10%-80%) strongly predicts views. The BODY matters more than the hook.

indicators.label: Body Slope Signal
indicators.notes: r=+0.362 vs log(views). Flatter body slope (less negative decay through middle 70%) = more views. Also r=+0.228 vs keep. Smooth retention = algorithm fuel.

indicators.label: End Retention Signal
indicators.notes: r=+0.320 vs log(views), r=+0.958 vs retention. End retention (at 80% mark) strongly predicts both views and average retention.

indicators.label: Early Slope Views
indicators.notes: r=-0.291 vs log(views). Gentler early slope (less steep drop in first 10%) correlates with more views. But individual ret_at_10pct is only r=+0.06.

indicators.label: End Drop Keep
indicators.notes: r=-0.389 vs keep. END DROP is the #1 keep predictor from retention shapes. Videos where audience bails at ending get lower keep. Don't let videos cliff at the end.

indicators.label: End Bump Keep
indicators.notes: has_end_bump r=+0.289 vs keep. Re-engagement bumps in last 20% predict higher keep. Payoff/reveal at end brings people back.

indicators.label: Early Hook Weak For Views
indicators.notes: ret_at_10pct r=+0.06, ret_at_3pct r=+0.12 vs views. Early hook retention is a WEAK predictor of total views. Body and endgame matter far more.

indicators.label: Cluster3 Ideal Shape
indicators.notes: Cluster 3 (51 videos, 12.4M avg): loses 31% in first 10% then HOLDS. Body slope nearly flat. Includes INDESTRUCTIBLE (285M), MAGNET shoes (55M). THE winning shape.

indicators.label: Cluster5 Hook Trap
indicators.notes: Cluster 5 (15 videos, 1.6M avg): BEST early retention (0.807 at 10%) but end cliff (0.499→0.277). Great hook can't save bad ending. Hook obsession is a trap.

indicators.label: Body Over Hook Thesis
indicators.notes: STRATEGIC: Don't obsess over hook — obsess over body and ending. Body signals (stability r=0.365, slope r=0.362) dwarf hook signals (ret_10pct r=0.06) for predicting views.

indicators.label: New Signals 57Pct Lift
indicators.notes: Model F (stepwise 14 features) CV R²=0.297 vs Model A (original 7) CV R²=0.189. +57% relative lift. New concept/narrative/audio signals genuinely improve prediction.

indicators.label: Stepwise Best Model
indicators.notes: Best model: 14 features, CV R²=0.297, RMSE=0.582. Top features by entry order: keep, n_categories, retention, duration_s, is_GIANT, is_MAKING, has_clear_payoff, has_hook_question.

indicators.label: Concept Categories Biggest Gain
indicators.notes: Concept categories (n_categories, is_MAKING, is_INDESTRUCTIBLE) are the biggest new contributors. Entered steps 2,5,6,10 in stepwise. Content WHAT > content HOW.

indicators.label: Narrative Signals Incremental
indicators.notes: has_clear_payoff and has_hook_question each add incremental R². Narrative structure helps but less than concept category. Payoff > hook in importance.

indicators.label: Audio Proxy Modest
indicators.notes: key_word_repeat_count and lexical_diversity_first30s add modest lift. Audio proxies contribute last in stepwise — real but small signal.

indicators.label: Kitchen Sink Overfits
indicators.notes: 37-feature kitchen sink model: full R²=0.475 but CV R²=0.007. Massive overfit. Stepwise selection essential at n=203. Never use all features.

indicators.label: Gbm No Help
indicators.notes: Best GBM (stepwise features): CV R²=0.253 vs linear CV R²=0.297. GBMs underperform linear at n=203. Non-linear effects don't help at this sample size.

indicators.label: Content Ceiling 30Pct
indicators.notes: Content-controllable signals ceiling at ~30% CV R². 70% of view variance is non-content (algorithm, timing, subscriber base, competition). Content gives edge, not guarantee.

indicators.label: Top Standardized Coefficients
indicators.notes: Standardized importance: retention(+0.305) > duration(+0.205) > keep(+0.177) > is_MAKING(+0.141) > key_word_repeat(+0.084) > hook_question(+0.081). Retention is king.

indicators.label: No Conversion Reach Tradeoff
indicators.notes: sub_conversion_rate r=+0.027 vs views (p=0.70). NO TRADEOFF. Viral videos convert at same rate as small ones. Absolute subs gained scales perfectly with views (r=+0.943 log-log).

indicators.label: Superhero Build Best Converter
indicators.notes: SUPERHERO_BUILD category converts at 0.194% — 73% better than worst (VEHICLE_MACHINE 0.112%). MAKING_BUILD 0.161%, INDESTRUCTIBLE 0.156%.

indicators.label: Duration Drives Conversion
indicators.notes: Duration partial_r=+0.302 vs conversion (p=0.000013), controlling for views. #1 predictor. Longer videos = more time to convert = higher sub rate. Growth engines are 26% longer.

indicators.label: Concept Density Conversion
indicators.notes: concept_density partial_r=+0.252 vs conversion (p=0.00029). Packing more recognizable concepts into a video drives subscription independent of views.

indicators.label: Aggressive Filtering Converts
indicators.notes: Retention partial_r=-0.252, keep partial_r=-0.175 vs conversion. LOWER retention = BETTER conversion. Videos that filter casual viewers but deeply engage survivors convert more subs.

indicators.label: Universal Appeal Retains Subs
indicators.notes: sub_retention_gap r=+0.301 vs churn (p=0.000014). Videos where subs retain much more than non-subs = higher churn. "Insider" content attracts subs who leave. Universal appeal retains.

indicators.label: Retention Shape Null Conversion
indicators.notes: All retention curve shape metrics (body, end, slope, flatness) show r<|0.10| vs conversion. Retention SHAPE doesn't matter for subscriptions — only duration and content concept do.

indicators.label: Growth Engine Formula
indicators.notes: Growth engine DNA: 60+ seconds, concept-dense (SUPERHERO/MAKING), high hook loading, universal appeal (low sub/nonsub gap), moderate cognitive load. Views don't hurt conversion — maximize both.

indicators.label: Conversion Model R2
indicators.notes: Multiple regression for conversion: R²=0.224. Top predictors: net_novelty(+), keep(-), duration(+), novelty(-). 78% of conversion variance is external (brand/personality/timing).

indicators.label: Hypothesized Combos Null
indicators.notes: All 8 pre-theorized interaction hypotheses FAILED (none p<0.05). No magic combos — body_stability×MAKING, conflict×stability, etc. are all additive, not multiplicative.

indicators.label: Arc X Questions Synergy
indicators.notes: arc_score × questions_first30s: dR²=+3.69%, p=0.004. BEST CONTROLLABLE SYNERGY. Strong narrative arc + question hooks = super-additive views. Do BOTH.

indicators.label: Keep X Conflict Synergy
indicators.notes: keep × total_conflict: dR²=+3.48%, p=0.003. High keep + high conflict = multiplicative. Conflict only pays off if the hook gets people in first.

indicators.label: Keep X Arc Synergy
indicators.notes: keep × arc_score: dR²=+3.41%, p=0.003. Good hook + complete narrative arc = super-additive. Story payoff multiplies the hook's effect.

indicators.label: Fast Hook Cancels Arc
indicators.notes: hook_pace × arc_score: beta=-0.120, dR²=+3.36%, p=0.007. ANTAGONISTIC. Fast hooks CANCEL narrative arc benefits. These are COMPETING strategies, not complementary.

indicators.label: Fast Hook Cancels Conflict
indicators.notes: hook_pace × total_conflict: beta=-0.103, dR²=+2.12%, p=0.030. Fast pace undermines conflict payoff. Slow, deliberate delivery lets conflict land.

indicators.label: Signals Mostly Additive
indicators.notes: Of 105 pairs tested, only 19 had significant interactions. Most content signals contribute INDEPENDENTLY. No need for complex interaction models — additive is sufficient.

indicators.label: Trajectory Dominates Interactions
indicators.notes: Top 5 interactions by dR² all involve trajectory signals (day1_views, day7_vs_day1, day1_pct). But these are outcomes, not controllable. Content interactions are weaker but actionable.

indicators.label: Two Strategies Compete
indicators.notes: TWO COMPETING STRATEGIES: (A) Slow hook + narrative arc + conflict density, or (B) Fast hook + simple content. Mixing A and B cancels benefits. Pick one lane.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Acceleration
indicators.notes: MEGA videos accelerate 7.7× in first 3 days (d=+2.26, p=0.03) vs 1.8× for underperformers. Week-1 growth: 102× vs 1.1×. THE defining trajectory signature.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Duration
indicators.notes: MEGAs are 66% longer: 78s avg vs 47s for underperformers (d=+1.50). Duration is a structural advantage for both views and subscriber conversion.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Exclamations
indicators.notes: MEGAs have 3.5× more exclamations per minute (d=+1.41, p=0.02). EVERY mega-hit has above-average energy (avg z=+1.27). Energy is a universal mega-hit trait.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Questions Hook
indicators.notes: 60% of MEGAs have hook questions vs 15% of underperformers (d=+1.19). questions_first30s d=+1.44. Asking questions in first 30s is a mega-hit marker.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Conflict
indicators.notes: MEGAs average 8.2 conflict events vs 4.5 for underperformers (d=+1.35). More conflict + more surprise moments (d=+1.25). Conflict density separates tiers.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Arc Score
indicators.notes: arc_score d=+1.14 (p=0.04). MEGAs have significantly stronger narrative arcs. Confirms arc_score as a real discriminator, not just correlation noise.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Vz Score
indicators.notes: vz_score d=+1.11 (p=0.02). MEGAs have higher visual Zeigarnik — despite vz_score being weak in linear correlation (r=0.016), it DISCRIMINATES at the extreme tier level.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Slow Hook
indicators.notes: ALL mega-hits share: low words/first30s (z=-0.71), low wps_first30s (z=-0.71), low wps_overall (z=-0.65). Slow, deliberate delivery is universal mega-hit DNA.

indicators.label: Mega Dna End Holds
indicators.notes: ALL mega-hits share: high end_slope (z=+0.58), low end_drop (z=-0.54). Retention holds at the end. No cliff endings. Confirms body_over_hook thesis.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Familiar Setting
indicators.notes: environment_novelty z=-0.52 for ALL megas. Familiar settings (workshop, not exotic). Novel CONCEPT in familiar ENVIRONMENT is the formula.

indicators.label: Mega Dna Checklist 10
indicators.notes: MEGA DNA CHECKLIST: (1) high exclamations, (2) high vz_score, (3) slow hook words, (4) slow hook pace, (5) slow overall pace, (6) high end_slope, (7) no second peak, (8) low end_drop, (9) familiar setting, (10) low day1_pct.

indicators.label: Near Miss Videos
indicators.notes: NEAR-MISSES (mega DNA, low views): "difference is crazy" DNA+2.09→1.8M, "hidden blade" +0.81→546K, "Doc Oc suit" +0.77→791K. Right content DNA but external factors failed.

indicators.label: Surprise Hits
indicators.notes: SURPRISE HITS (weak DNA, high views): "circle 24hrs" DNA-0.58→18.9M, "CHINCHILLA" -0.52→24.2M, "chocolate bar" -0.51→20.7M. External factors (timing, algorithm) can override content.

indicators.label: Dna Score Tier Gradient
indicators.notes: DNA score by tier: MEGA +0.625, HIT +0.104, SOLID +0.147, AVERAGE -0.038, UNDERPERFORM -0.155. Clear gradient but wide overlap — DNA is necessary but not sufficient.

indicators.label: 13 Monotonic Signals
indicators.notes: 13 signals scale monotonically across ALL 5 tiers: day1_views, day growth ratios, avg_wm_per_view, questions_hook, total_conflict, conflict_count, hook_question, hook_pace(inv), intensity_words, you_density(inv).

indicators.label: Previous Video Momentum
indicators.notes: r=+0.354 vs log(views). Previous video's views is the strongest temporal predictor. Success breeds success — momentum is real.

indicators.label: Rolling 5 Momentum
indicators.notes: r=+0.282 vs log(views). Rolling 5-video average performance predicts next video. Sustained momentum matters more than single hits.

indicators.label: Halo Effect 2X
indicators.notes: Videos after mega-hits (>17.97M) avg 4.3M vs 1.9M after normal videos — 2.30× multiplier. Halo effect is real and large. Algorithm spillover.

indicators.label: Delayed Followup Better
indicators.notes: After mega-hit, waiting >3 days yields 5.0M avg vs 3.3M for quick followup. Delay captures MORE halo — algorithm still promoting hit while new video drops.

indicators.label: Hot Streak Modest
indicators.notes: After 3+ consecutive above-median videos: 3.7M avg vs 1.4M without streak (2.58×). Probability of staying above median: 56.7% vs 45.1%. Real but modest.

indicators.label: Calendar Timing Null
indicators.notes: Day of week r=+0.023, month r=+0.090, holiday distance r=-0.086, cadence r=-0.021. Calendar timing is NOISE. Don't optimize for upload day/time.

indicators.label: Upload Frequency Null
indicators.notes: Upload cadence r=-0.021. Whether 1-2/month or 6-10/month, performance is similar. Frequency doesn't help or hurt. Quality >>> quantity.

indicators.label: Channel Maturity Null
indicators.notes: Sequence position r=+0.054. No linear channel maturity effect. U-shape: early novelty + late audience both help, middle is flat.

indicators.label: Temporal Only Lever
indicators.notes: ONLY temporal lever: what you do AFTER a hit. 4-7 day gap is optimal (2.81M avg). Maintain momentum streaks. Calendar timing is negligible.

indicators.label: Gerund Plus Object Formula
indicators.notes: r=+0.282 vs log(views). "[Gerund] + [Object]" is the #1 title formula. "Making INDESTRUCTIBLE armour" > "I made armour". NEW strongest title signal, refines action_verb finding.

indicators.label: Impersonal Framing
indicators.notes: r=+0.257 vs log(views). Impersonal framing (no I/my/me) massively outperforms first-person. "Making shoes" >> "I made shoes". First-person r=-0.107 (hurts).

indicators.label: Object Count Signal
indicators.notes: r=+0.250 vs log(views). More named concrete objects in title = more views. has_object r=+0.222. Specificity drives clicks.

indicators.label: Power Plus Object
indicators.notes: r=+0.203 vs log(views). Power word + object combo ("INDESTRUCTIBLE armour", "BULLETPROOF shield") is a proven high-performance pattern.

indicators.label: All Caps Kills Keep
indicators.notes: all_caps_count r=-0.283 vs keep. STRONGEST keep signal from titles. Heavy ALL CAPS = clickbait expectation gap = lower retention. Caps ratio r=-0.226.

indicators.label: First Person Hurts
indicators.notes: first_person r=-0.107 vs views, r=-0.131 vs keep. "I/my/me" framing actively hurts both views and retention. Impersonal is better.

indicators.label: Gerund Object Cluster Wins
indicators.notes: GERUND_OBJECT cluster avg log_views=6.813 (n=28) vs FIRST_PERSON 6.135 (n=57). 4.8× more views for gerund+object formula. The formula gap is massive.

indicators.label: Mega Title Formula
indicators.notes: GERUND_OBJECT + POWER_OBJECT combined: avg log_views=7.094 (n=7). THE mega-formula. "Making/Testing/Building [POWER WORD] [object]".

indicators.label: First Word Climbing Best
indicators.notes: First word rankings: "climbing"=7.406, "testing"=6.777, "making"=6.707, "walking"=6.498, "this"=5.789 (worst). Leading verb choice matters.

indicators.label: Object Armour Shoes Top
indicators.notes: Object rankings: "armour"=7.664, "helmet"=6.772, "shoes"=6.757 (n=20!), "suit"=6.493. Wearable/protective objects dominate.

indicators.label: Price Best Retention
indicators.notes: PRICE_OBJECT cluster: avg retention 86.1% — best of any formula. Dollar amounts sustain interest once clicked. Moderate views but highest engagement.

indicators.label: Title Nulls Confirmed
indicators.notes: Dollar amounts r=-0.001, emojis r=+0.025, questions r=-0.015, measurements r=-0.002, word count r=-0.015 vs views. All effectively null. Don't optimize these.

indicators.label: Playbook 1 Body Over Hook
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #1: Body retention (10-80%) is 6× more predictive than hook. body_stability r=+0.365 vs ret_at_10pct r=+0.06. Cluster 3 (hold after drop) = 12.4M avg.

indicators.label: Playbook 2 Indestructible Making
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #2: INDESTRUCTIBLE+MAKING = 143.8M avg (18× dataset). Triple combo with hook_question + keep>75 = 84.7M avg, 80% hit rate.

indicators.label: Playbook 3 Start Slow
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #3: 3-4 WPS in first 10s, then accelerate. ALL mega-hits have slow hooks (z=-0.71). Fast hooks cancel narrative arc benefits (antagonistic p=0.007).

indicators.label: Playbook 4 Ask Questions
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #4: Question in first 30s r=+0.215. 100% of 100M+ videos have question in first 10s. 60% of megas vs 15% underperformers. But be SPECIFIC, not formulaic.

indicators.label: Playbook 5 Narrative Arc
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #5: 3/4 narrative elements (hook+conflict+escalation+payoff) = 13.0M avg vs 0/4 = 1.4M. Use "but" pivots. Diminishing returns at full 4/4.

indicators.label: Playbook 6 Duration 45 60S
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #6: 45-60s = 28% hit 10M+ rate. Under 30s = 2% (death zone). Mega avg 78s. Duration also #1 conversion predictor (partial_r=+0.302).

indicators.label: Playbook 7 Simple Language
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #7: lexical_diversity r=-0.333 (strongest retention signal). Repeat key words 3×. Action verbs have accelerating returns. Avoid "you/your" (r=-0.298 vs keep).

indicators.label: Playbook 8 Retention Shape
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #8: Big early drop OK (replays r=+0.363), then hold FLAT, end strong (end_drop r=-0.389 vs keep). End bumps add +0.289 keep.

indicators.label: Playbook 9 Switch Concepts
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #9: Concept switching = 2.5× lift. Vary from recent avg. After hit, 33% next video hits too. Don't chain same concept — variety wins.

indicators.label: Playbook 10 Keep 75 Gateway
indicators.notes: PLAYBOOK #10: Keep ≥75 is gateway to algorithmic reach (r=+0.476). Returns are LINEAR — every point matters. Below 68: rarely breaks out. At 80+: 41% hit 10M+.

indicators.label: Grand Synthesis Formula
indicators.notes: FORMULA: Content Score = keep + (1-body_drop)×30 + body_stability×20 + hook_body_ratio×5 + arc_score×10 + questions_first30s×15 + action_density×25 - opening_wps×5. CV R²=0.297.

indicators.label: Grand Synthesis Ceiling
indicators.notes: GRAND CEILING: 30% content-explainable, 70% external (algorithm, timing, momentum). Content gives edge not guarantee. 913 total discoveries analyzed across 10 categories.

indicators.label: Two Strategies Final
indicators.notes: TWO STRATEGIES (never mix): A=Slow hook + narrative arc + conflict (RECOMMENDED). B=Fast hook + simple content. Mixing is ANTAGONISTIC. Pick A for mega-hit potential.

indicators.label: Title Mega Formula Final
indicators.notes: TITLE FORMULA: "[Gerund] [POWER WORD] [concrete object]". Impersonal framing. No ALL CAPS spam. No "I/my". Example: "Making INDESTRUCTIBLE armour" (285M views).

Drop visuals: product/process shots without human anchor

BREAKTHROUGH: Videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking videos (5.2M vs 530K median)
Retention GAIN words: visceral/physical (painful +5.9%, difference +4.3%, hurt +2.5%) — SHOW don't TELL
Retention DROP words: explanatory transitions (so, -11.2%, however, -8.6%) are #1 retention killer
Visual: physical_challenge +12% at peaks, product/food shots cause drops without human anchor

Curve diff at 80-90%: late_peakers=+0.061, early_peakers=-0.072, spread=13.3% — the ending makes or breaks the video

Payoff language at climax: "finally" "hours" "time" at peak. Setup language at hook: "decided" "wanted" "started". Setup ≠ payoff
Hook type: "can_x" = 3.1M median views (best). "see_what_happens" = 2.4M + best retention (+3.1%). "i_did_x" = 1.3M (worst)
Action verb opening: 4.2M median views vs 1.6M for non-action. "train"=16.2M, "walk"=6.7M, "I"=1.0M. Never start with "I"
Opening density: 6-10 words in first 3s = 2.9M (optimal). Sparse (<5) = 105K. Dense (11-15) = 1.6M. Don't overpack the hook
Segment analysis: call_to_action = -4.1% delta (WORST, 4x worse than any other). Resolution=+3.1%, Climax=+3.0% (BEST). Never CTA mid-video
Segment gains: resolution (+3.1%), climax (+3.0%), complication (+2.8%), execution (+2.8%). Payoff > setup for retention
Concept retention: fitness/body challenges retain best (+10.1%). Building/construction (-11.3%) and water (-9.4%) retain worst
indestructible concept: -0.9% retention BUT 15.5M median views — proving views ≠ retention. Some concepts get clicks despite poor hold.
Recovery rate after retention drop = 4.3%. Once viewers leave, they're gone. Prevention > recovery. Every pause is a point of no return.
so, appears 22.5x more in bottom 50 vs top 50 videos. Single strongest vocabulary differentiator between success and failure.
Top video vocab: happen(6x), crazy(5x), feel(3.8x), feet(4.7x). Bottom: so,(22.5x), suit(12x), it,(12x), able(6.5x), back(6x)
Segment structure: top videos avg 5.2 segments (simpler), bottom 5.7. Top path: hook→setup→experiment→conclusion. Bottom adds indirection.
Script retention predictor: r=0.25 with above_baseline. Top quintile=3.55M views, bottom=907K (4x gap from script alone)
Feature importance: drop_density (r=-0.15) > so_count (r=-0.12) > avg_word_score (r=+0.11) > late_loading (r=+0.08)
Cadence: sents_per_sec r=0.854 with retention — STRONGEST single feature. Short rapid sentences (6-10 words, 0.35+/sec) = best retention
Sentence length: 6-10 words optimal (avg_ab=-0.004, 3.7M views). 15-20 words = terrible (avg_ab=-0.101). Keep sentences SHORT.
Low alternation (<30% variation) = +0.320 avg_ab (BEST). High alternation (70%+) = -0.055. Consistent short beats varied length.
Stable sentence length across video (+0.185 avg_ab) beats both accelerating and decelerating patterns. Don't vary the rhythm.
Pre-event: 'finally'/'world's'/'end' appear 92% exclusively before retention peaks — strongest peak predictor words
Pre-event: 'wait'/'won't'/'check'/'videos' appear 75-89% before drops — CTA/meta language is the #1 drop predictor
Trigram 'i won't make you wait, full videos on...' appears 7x before drops vs 0x before peaks — THE drop pattern
Trigram 'this is what I looked like' appears 11x before peaks — THE reveal/payoff pattern
Positive 2nd derivative (acceleration) predicts peaks 87.6% of the time, 3 steps ahead. Peaks have MOMENTUM.
Drops have characteristic shock signature: -0.14 accel at t-2, +0.14 bounce at t-1, then collapse. No warning.
Views-weighted above_baseline builds MONOTONICALLY 0→99%. High-view videos NEVER stop improving vs average.
peak→peak transitions = 36.5% (most common). Drop recovery takes 60% of video vs 40% to lose a peak.
Reaction frames = 29% of peaks vs 10% of drops (+19% diff). STRONGEST visual differentiator.
Demonstration frames = 30% of drops vs 17% of peaks. Process shots lose viewers.
Wide shots = 21% of peaks vs 0% of drops. Wide shots = reveal moments. Medium shots = neutral/process.
High energy frames = 37.5% peaks vs 20% drops. Energy level correlates with retention.
Drop predictor words (wait/check/full/video/won't) appear ONLY in Q4 — ALL drops from CTA language are end-of-video
always_above videos: 1.4 peaks at avg 54%, 4.2M views. always_below: 2.1 peaks at avg 30%, 498K views. Bottom has MORE events but EARLIER.
Recovery rate after drop = 17.4%. When recovery happens: avg 2.1% gap (1 second). After drop, setup language = NO recovery ever.
60-80% peak trajectory: SMOOTH continuous build from +0.058→+0.073 over 15 steps. No dips. Already above average the entire approach.
always_above opens with action ('see','try','walk'). always_below opens with results ('50,000','finished'). Don't front-load the payoff.
Building videos use "something you've always" (anticipation). Bottom use "50 000" and "finally finished" (achievement stated upfront).
'make' becomes 2x worse as a drop word in Q4 (Q1=-0.073, Q4=-0.151). Meta-language toxicity amplifies with video position.
pure_build arc = 6.65M median views (#1). late_build = 3.48M (#2). pure_decay = 653K (#7). valley = 494K (worst).
Top 20%: 52.7% are builders (pure+late). Bottom 20%: 56.8% are chaotic/decaying. 0% of top are pure_decay.
Gap between top 20% and bottom 20% WIDENS throughout video: 0.8% gap at start → 16.4% gap at end. ENDING IS EVERYTHING.
Golden 5% (mega-hits): 50% late_build, 22% dip_then_build. They START below average (5-30%) then BUILD. Crossover at 40%.
Golden 5% at 95-99%: +8.0% above baseline while rest is -0.4%. Final 5% of video is THE differentiator for mega-virality.
Phase 1 vocab: top says 'does/24/shoes' (action/specific). Bottom says '50/i've/followers' (numbers/self-reference).
Phase 4 vocab: top says 'getting/came/almost/before' (approaching climax). Bottom says 'i've/water/gonna' (resignation/vague).
mountain arc (mean 22.8M, median 3.1M) — contains mega-hits with extreme upside but higher variance. Build→peak→decline.
Statement sentences at peaks (44%) vs drops (35%). Quantitative at drops (34%) vs peaks (24%). Assertions retain, data dumps drop.
Questions more common at drops (22%) than peaks (13%). Uncertain narrative = viewer exit. Be assertive, not questioning.
Peak sentence template: "I wanted to see if these [X]" (challenge). Drop template: "This is an [X]" (explanation). Challenge > explain.
Phase transitions (20/40/60/80%) dominated by CONTRAST sentences (31-37% vs 5% at events). Contrast = narrative glue between phases.
